name: Asian Incorporated
description_short: Non-profit organization providing business development, affordable housing, and financial empowerment services to Asian American and low-income communities across Northern California.
description_long:
ASIAN, Inc. is a non-profit organization based in Northern California that has operated for over five decades, focusing on economic and social equity for Asian Americans and other socioeconomically disadvantaged communities. The organization's mission centers on empowering economic independence through three core service areas: business development, affordable housing, and advocacy.

ASIAN, Inc. offers comprehensive services across multiple regions. Their business centers in San Francisco, San Jose, and Fresno provide business assistance, development, and supplier diversity certifications to small and micro-businesses. For housing, they deliver affordable rental management, housing counseling, homeownership programs, and foreclosure prevention services. The organization also engages in policy advocacy and community coalition building focused on social and economic justice for AANHPI (Asian American, Native Hawaiian, and Pacific Islander) communities.

The organization distinguishes itself through measurable community impact and multilingual, multicultural service delivery. They have assisted over 10,000 small and micro-business clients, secured $1B+ in contracts and $900M+ in funding since 2007, developed over 1,000 affordable housing units, and helped 500+ clients purchase their first home since 1999. Their board consists of established business and community leaders, and they maintain transparency through published annual reports.

As a non-profit, ASIAN, Inc. operates differently from commercial finance companies—their services are subsidized or free, with no profit motive. However, their affordable housing and homeownership programs may have income eligibility requirements, and their geographic service area is primarily limited to Northern California with business centers in specific cities.
pros: Over 50 years of established history serving Bay Area and Northern California communities
Comprehensive services across business development, housing, and financial empowerment in one organization
Documented impact: 10,000+ small business clients, $1B+ in contracts secured, 1,000+ affordable housing units developed
Multilingual and multicultural service delivery approach
Non-profit structure ensures services prioritize community benefit over profit
Transparent governance with published board information and annual reports
Free or low-cost services, unlike commercial lenders
cons: Geographic service area limited primarily to Northern California (San Francisco, San Jose, Fresno regions)
No information provided on specific loan terms, APR rates, or detailed program eligibility requirements on public website
Affordable housing programs appear to have income and eligibility restrictions not fully detailed online
Website does not clearly specify which services are free versus fee-based
No online application or direct service access mechanisms described on website
best_for: Asian American and low-to-moderate income entrepreneurs seeking business development support in Northern California
First-time homebuyers and homeowners in San Francisco Bay Area seeking affordable housing or foreclosure prevention
Small business owners needing supplier diversity certifications and business financing guidance
diagnosis: ASIAN, Inc. is best for low-to-moderate-income individuals and small business owners in Northern California seeking non-profit, community-focused financial assistance with no predatory lending practices. The main caveat is that services are geographically limited to Northern California regions and require eligibility verification; prospective clients should contact directly for specific program details, eligibility requirements, and service availability.
services: Business center assistance and business development consulting
Small and micro-business financing guidance and contract support
Homeownership programs and first-time buyer assistance
Community development credit union formation assistance
Policy advocacy and social justice coalition building
Community organizing and economic development partnerships
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1167 Mission Street, 4Th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94103
